epartment, University of California, Berkeley Technical Report No. UCB/EECS-2013-65 May 15, 2013 http://www2.eecs.berkeley.edu/Pubs/TechRpts/2013/EECS-2013-65.pdf This paper discusses the use of the Ptides model of computation as a coordinat

of the Ptides model of computation as a coordination language for the design of deterministic, event-driven, real-time, distributed embedded systems. Specifically, the paper shows how the use of synchronized clocks in the context of Ptides enables e

hronized clocks in the context of Ptides enables explicit, platform independent specification of functionality and timing. From this specification, we generate code for two target platforms: Renesas and XMOS. The generated code includes a lightweight

nd XMOS. The generated code includes a lightweight operating system which performs scheduling, I/O and network handling as well as application specific tasks. Ptides models are developed in Ptolemy, a design and simulation environment for heterogeneo

design and simulation environment for heterogeneous systems. This framework also contains a code generation framework which is leveraged to derive Ptides implementations from the models. We illustrate our approach by designing a simple Ptides applic

e our approach by designing a simple Ptides application, a small component in a printing press responsible for on-the-fly changeover between paper rolls. We demonstrate the design process and show that the generated code exhibits identical timing at
